items: Prepared for {{fact:f:E-2004-001.client}}
Presented to David Dyer, General Manager, Johnson PLC
Prepared by Charles Olson, Design Technician
Design team: Pamela Warren, Project Engineer; Jeffrey Perez, Design Technician
Work commenced {{fact:f:E-2004-001.start}}
Site walked and photographed; field notes are in the job file
Drainage areas delineated for existing and proposed conditions
Pre-development runoff computed for the design storms the ordinance names
Post-development computations under way; detention concept not yet fixed
No sheets started. We are still establishing what the site does.

blocks.text: What We Found on the Ground

items: The survey shows the site draining to one point. It drains to two.
The culvert under the access drive is partly silted and does not sit at the invert the survey gives it
The receiving ditch below the outfall is shallower than plotted and is on ground you do not control
None of this stops the design. It changes where the detention has to sit.
Charles Olson to re-shoot the outfall with our own level rather than take the survey's word for it
Detention sized to the outfall as measured, not as plotted
Pamela Warren will state every assumption in the report where the reviewer can find it
Jeffrey Perez will build the details and the quantity takeoff off the fixed outlet structure
Grading and drainage sheets follow once the pond has a volume
One sealed package: plan set, report, and erosion control sheets together
The property file: the old grading permit and anything on the existing culverts
The geotechnical report, if one was performed
Confirmation of the limits of the proposed improvements before we fix the grading
Notice of any change to the building footprint, which moves the runoff and the pond with it

blocks.text: We will complete the post-development computations and fix the detention concept, then bring the layout back to Johnson PLC before we commit it to sheets. We will contact your office to arrange that session. Design continues in the meantime on the items that do not depend on it.
